rules.title: PDSPrj is ZIP-style container
rules.rule: .pdsprj files begin with PK and contain internal project files such as PROJECT.XML, ROOT.DSN, ROOT.CDB, and SCRIPTS/PWRRAILS.DAT.

rules.title: ROOT.DSN controls visual object existence
rules.rule: Components visible in ROOT.DSN appear in Proteus even if ROOT.CDB is missing entries; CDB-only components do not create visible schematic objects.

rules.title: ROOT.CDB controls resistor values and references
rules.rule: For existing resistors, ROOT.CDB is authoritative for value and reference designator. ROOT.DSN display metadata can be normalized from ROOT.CDB on open/save.

rules.title: Terminal labels live in ROOT.DSN
rules.rule: INPUT, OUTPUT, POWER, and GROUND terminal objects and labels are stored in ROOT.DSN. Terminal-only changes do not alter ROOT.CDB.

rules.title: Topology is ROOT.DSN-controlled
rules.rule: Series/parallel/ladder topology changes are represented in ROOT.DSN through terminal labels, wire stubs, and object layout. ROOT.CDB can remain identical across different topologies with the same component refs/values.

rules.title: ROOT.CDB must exist
rules.rule: Removing ROOT.CDB entirely causes fatal Proteus/ISIS failure. A generator must include ROOT.CDB, even if minimal.

rules.title: PWRRAILS.DAT unchanged in early terminal/passive tests
rules.rule: Visible VCC/GND terminals and resistor networks did not modify SCRIPTS/PWRRAILS.DAT. For early generator versions, copy this file unchanged from a template.

rules.title: V9 resistor generator uses linked endpoint groups
rules.rule: For generated resistor networks, emit matching input/output terminal records, a resistor visual record, and two short-wire records with consistent link suffixes and only one final terminator at the end of the object stream.

rules.title: V9 resistor generation uses E001 as base
rules.rule: Use the blank E001 project as the .pdsprj container base for resistor generation. Donor projects may provide byte-record templates, but generated outputs replace ROOT.CDB and ROOT.DSN and copy PROJECT.XML/PWRRAILS from E001.

rules.title: Power and ground endpoint terminals are label-width constrained
rules.rule: For the locked resistor V9 method, use two-character labels such as V0 and G0. Power bridge labels and resistor terminal labels remain two-character; $TERGROUND is safe as a right endpoint replacement for $TEROUTPUT.

rules.title: Resistor visual rotation field
rules.rule: In the V9 resistor visual record, the four bytes after the final model placement x/y pair store rotation as a signed tenths-of-degrees value in the low 16 bits. 0 is horizontal; -900 is vertical down. Vertical generation must rotate terminal/wire endpoint positions along the same axis.

rules.title: Standalone visual wires are disabled in production resistor generation
evidence: user reported VGDVC.dll errors on parallel-and-later visual-wire cases
rules.rule: Do not emit standalone layout.visual_wires in the production resistor generator. Parse and record them as skipped until a Proteus-created donor establishes VGDVC-safe standalone wire records.

rules.title: Power nodes use donor-derived output bridge
rules.rule: The main resistor generator must emit one donor-derived $TERPOWER -> $TEROUTPUT(V0) bridge for the power node. Powered resistor endpoints stay ordinary $TERINPUT(V0) terminals so same-name terminal topology connects them to the bridge.

rules.title: Dense resistor layouts are stretched before generation
evidence: user reported overlapped generated resistor circuits
rules.rule: Treat manual component coordinates as placement hints. If repeated x/y positions are closer than the safe V9 grid, stretch them before emitting ROOT.DSN so terminal records, short wires, and resistor visual bodies do not overlap. The current production safe grid is 2540000 internal units on both x and y.

rules.title: Resistor generator is locked as main for current scope
rules.rule: Use src/proteusgen/resistor_v9.py as the main resistor generator. Do not reintroduce direct $TERPOWER resistor endpoints or standalone production visual wires. New component work starts with capacitor support.

rules.title: Capacitor work stays temporary until Proteus accepts guarded diagnostics
rules.rule: Do not promote capacitor generation to the main generator until exact one-cap CDB and CAP_T02 object reproduction, same-length patching, coordinate translation, and at least one multi-cap case open in Proteus without VGDVC/library errors.
